note: Closure records for gaps marked DONE. The Deployment Strategy Hardening wave (DS-*) was seeded 2026-07-09; DS-04 and DS-05 were closed 2026-07-09 with live G1 verification. New closures must carry a full closure record per the standard (id, closedAt, closureCommit, evidence, validationCommands, dependencyDisposition).

records.evidence: Dedup inbox wired on the tenant-projection consumer endpoint. Verified live in the G1 fan-out harness: the REAL TenantProjectionConsumer ran against a live RabbitMQ + PostgreSQL; one published TenantEvent fanned out to both the UMS (ums.tenant-projection) and Tracker (tracker.tenant-projection) endpoints. A redelivered message (same MessageId) was NOT re-applied — InboxState held 3 rows, not 4.
validationCommands: G1 live fan-out harness: publish one TenantEvent to the MMS exchange with a live RabbitMQ + PostgreSQL; assert an InboxState row is written on consume, then redeliver the same MessageId and assert InboxState stays at 3 rows (duplicate not re-applied).

records.evidence: Read-check-write upsert replaced by a single set-based version-conditional statement (ON CONFLICT (tenant_id) DO UPDATE ... WHERE version < EXCLUDED.version). Verified live in the G1 fan-out harness against a real PostgreSQL: an out-of-order seq=2 was discarded while seq=1->seq=3 applied; the UMS projection DB ended at Version=3 'ACME v3' (matching Tracker's).
validationCommands: G1 live fan-out harness: apply seq=1, then out-of-order seq=2 (older) and seq=3 against a live PostgreSQL; assert seq=2 is discarded and masterdata.tenant_projection ends at Version=3 'ACME v3'.
